entry.title: Remove Assembled components DPP DMC
entry.content:
Hi,

I tried designing Production process to disassemble the assembled components. Its throwing an error saying "component not assembled" but the component is already assembled. 

Anyone faced this issue?

entry.title: Re: Webinar: SAP Business One - Uso Académico
entry.content:
Tiene interés en conocer más sobre nuestra solución SAP Business One para uso académico vía SAP University Alliances? Consulte esta info-sheet de ACC Sierre, Suiza. 

Y por cualquier otra consulta escríbannos ✍️ a sapnextgen@sap.com

entry.title: Re: GR error Cost element is not assigned to a cost compo...
entry.content:
• There are some similar KBAs available in OSS for transactions MM01 or CKMM, did you already check them?
  • Did you already look for forgotten transport requests (e.g. related to OKZT)

entry.title: Client Error: IF_HTTP_CLIENT object caused HTTP receive error: HTTP_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 I have created a custom class and i am calling the API of Maintenance order operation to get the operation data when i use GET Method  its working when i use POST Method its giving me below error . Its not fetching the token while POST. 

Client Error: IF_HTTP_CLIENT object caused HTTP receive error: HTTP_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE

if anyone faced the issue before can you please help and guide.
Thanks for your help
Nitin

entry.title: BTP Job Scheduling of different services
entry.content:
Hello, 

i have two CAP-Services (ODATA v4) on BTP CloudFoundry with some actions.
On both services i will call actions of the odata - service with jobscheduler.
For one service it works properly. But if i add a binding to the second service the scheduled jobs get error with JWT-Token.
So my question is: Is it possible to call actions on more than one service with one JobScheduler-Instance and if yes, what can be my error?

entry.title: Smart Data Streaming - KAFKA Adapter - Importing JSON array
entry.content:
Hello Gurus, 

My Question is regarding JSON Input Kafka Adapter. I want to know the possibility to import JSON array form kafka adapter. Sample JSON want to import from kafka adapter.


"items":
[
{
"item‑id":"10",
"item‑start‑date":"2024‑08‑07T07:00:00",
"item‑end‑date":"2025‑08‑07T06:59:59",
"sales_ord_num":"1234545457",
"quantity":12
},
{
"item‑id":"20",
"item‑start‑date":"2024‑08‑07T07:00:00",
"item‑end‑date":"2025‑08‑07T06:59:59",
"sales_ord_num":"1234545458",
"quantity":12
},
{
"item‑id":"30",
"item‑start‑date":"2024‑08‑07T07:00:00",
"item‑end‑date":"2025‑08‑07T06:59:59",
"sales_ord_num":"1234545459",
"quantity":12
},
{
"item‑id":"40",
"item‑start‑date":"2024‑08‑07T07:00:00",
"item‑end‑date":"2025‑08‑07T06:59:59",
"sales_ord_num":"1234545455",
"quantity":12
},
{
"item‑id":"50",
"item‑start‑date":"2024‑08‑07T07:00:00",
"item‑end‑date":"2025‑08‑07T06:59:59",
"sales_ord_num":"1234545454",
"quantity":12
}
]

How I can import all 5 Lines from array items[] into Input Stream. Can any one please suggest.

entry.title: Re: OData services not available in VSCODE
entry.content:
Debug Output of generator shows 401 errors:

WARN: Services unavailable from system: https://xxxxxxxx-0d9f-436b-xxxx-793b3ecaf3b0.abap.us10.hana.ondemand.com. OData version(s): V2, Catalog Request Type: LEGACY. Authentication incorrect! Error: Request failed with status code 401

entry.title: Re: Net price in PO after exchange but in different currency
entry.content: @raymond_giuseppi- PR  was in PLN

entry.title: Re: Malware Scanner in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Thanks for your reply. We have contacted SAP Consultant and he was reverting back the same just 2 days ago. But we have observed, that the virus has been detected using the URL you have shared, but Integration Suite doesn't detected it - my SAP Consultant has created an incident internally already to get this fixed.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Dear @David_Chaviano

I am a man , my name is bahaa and I'm excited to be part of the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I work for an organization called Edraky,
which is focused on providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. I'm writing to apply for certification  Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionC_S4CPB.

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload using RAP: Part -3
entry.content:
@amitgoyal @rex 

For this error, check whether the npm i xlsx command was given from main project folder .For me the error is gone when I ran it from user/projects instead of the current app folder.

entry.title: Re: How to get URL parameters in SAP Screen Personas
entry.content:
Yes,

SAP Screen Personas scripting doesn't have any objects to operate with URL. 
BUT you can use Javascript and window object to retrieve needed parameter. 

Try this:

/* globals console, window, URLSearchParams */
const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
console.log(params.get('sap-theme'));

If this doesn't work for you, search similar solution.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: special Keyfigure calculation in BW query designer
entry.content:
Hello,

there are multiple possible ways to do this. For a simple use case, you could duplicate the keyfigure selection and have it be cumulated in the selection properties. 

Another option would be to use a CURRENT MEMBER Variable for your time dimension and restrict your  keyfigure on an interval from the first month of the year to the current one for each row using said variable. Check out a more detailed description to solve your problem with CURRENT MEMBER here and here(YTD analysis).

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I’m an SAP Business One administrator based in Sialkot, Pakistan, and I hold a degree in computer science. I'm eager to enhance my expertise within the SAP ecosystem, especially in SAP Analytics Cloud. The digital skills initiative by SAP Learning presents a wonderful opportunity for individuals like me to grow and develop. I’m excited about the prospect of taking on this challenge and pursuing SAP Certification.

Thank you for considering my interest in this initiative.

entry.title: Re: Malware Scanner in Integration Suite

entry.title: Re: SAP IDM/SAC Support for SAC roles and TEAM liaison in IDM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have similar requirement using IPS and SAC.

However we are stuck is 2 issues:

Issue 1: When Version 1 is used - We have issue that the user is not able to login to SAC after the id is created in SAC. The user id needs to be deactivated and activated again in SAC and then it starts working.

Issue 2: When Version 2 is used - The team gets created in SAC, user gets tagged. But the SAC roles tagged to team gets detagged.

 

Can someone please assist how this can be fixed?

 

Regards,

Sayed Zubair

entry.title: Re: Malware Scanner in Integration Suite
entry.content: Yes exactly 🙂

entry.title: Re: GR error Cost element is not assigned to a cost compo...
entry.content: Hello Raymond, Thanks for yoour reply. The OKZT settings in Quality and Production are the same. GR posting is possible in Quality, but not in Production.

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Instance sharing | 'reference' service plan
entry.content:
Hi @antoniojmnunes ,

excellent blog on the topic. We looked into PostgreSQL instance sharing last June/July but were unsuccessful. Now that it's been added to the official documentation, and with your step-by-step, we would very much like to pursue this path and be able to share the instance across accounts.

We are, however, unable to add the 'reference' entitlement in any of our accounts, it's just not there. We have a Partners Global account with a Pay-As-You-Go pricing option. Could this be the reason? Maybe a roll out hasn't yet been done for every account type?

Please let us know if you have some input on this. Thanks!

BR,

Gonçalo

entry.title: Re: Hands-On Tutorial: Predictive Planning
entry.content:
Hello,

where can I download the csv file?

Thanks 🙂

entry.title: AUTO height details section
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using Crystal Reports to make a Sales Quotation layout.

In the DETAILS section, i have the lines of the sales quotation.

In the REPORT FOOTER section, i have subtotals, VAT, discounts...

The porblem is with the number of items per page.

I have a quotation with 57 lines.

In the report, i get HEADER, 57 lines and PAGE FOOTER in the first page, and the second page has HEADER, FOOTERS and a big blank space between both, since there are no more lines to print.

I can change the height of DETAILS saction manually, but i will get the same problem with a 40 lines sales quotation, for example.

What i want to do is make a variable field / section height depending on the number of lines.
Is it possible?

Thanks.

Victor

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Welcome to the SAP Community Group – Pune! 🚀
entry.content: Thanks @Punith_Oswal. I’m excited to be part of this community and look forward to many more memorable events.

entry.title: Re: Hands-On Tutorial: Predictive Planning
entry.content:
Hi @YvonneT 

The data file used in this article can be found here: https://github.com/antoinechabert/predictive/blob/master/product_forecast_planning.csv. Enjoy!

Best 

Antoine

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP: How to set up alert on database resident mem...
entry.content: Have you checked the functionality that you could get by using SAP Cloud ALM for HANA Cloud at https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/health-monitoring/health-mon-content.html?anchorId=section?

entry.title: Payslip read out loud for visually impaired
entry.content:
I am managing an implementation of EC and payroll where my customer has stumbled upon issues with read out loud functionality for payslips. If for example "-" and a number has a space between them, it is not read out as a negative number, which is what they would like. An account number will also be read out like a immensely bit number instead of being grouped in a way that make sense or just as a sequence of separate digits. I wonder if anybody knows existing solutions to this issue, or whether or not SAP is paying attention to ensuring that documents generated will work with immersive readers or browser plugins.

HCM Payroll

entry.title: Re: EWM PI Creation Error - No items Passed on
entry.content: Hi,On the PI_CREATE screen after entering the bin detail you are supposed to press the set flag before save. Click on the set flag which will change the status to green and then you can save it

entry.title: Re: Import into node dev_node failed. - Error during client creation: Not Found
entry.content: You have to provide more details. With that little information it will not be possible to help you.

Want to see what we have lined up for the year? Explore our page to learn more and register for our upcoming Espresso Sessions here: https://events.sap.com/sap-energy-utilities-espresso-sessions/en/home

entry.title: Re: i need to update the tax code for all or multiple line items without doing so manually
entry.content:
Hi,

Using Tax Code Determination, you can set default tax code for all items.  No need to select tax code here.  Once you set default tax code then SAP is auto takes tax code at row level.

entry.title: Re: Sort code for UK in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello,

you will need to maintain this information in the Bank Directory. Using the Bank directory, you will then create the my bank.


Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Re: Eclipse, Field for creation user is missing in source...
entry.content: hi gojila1029, did you activate (Ctrl + F3) again after?

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Choosing the Ideal SAP Warehousing Solution for Your Company
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain landscape, businesses require efficient and flexible warehouse management systems (WMS) to stay competitive. SAP offers robust solutions for optimizing warehouse operations. However, with multiple options available, such as Basic EWM, Advanced EWM, Decentralized EWM, and newer alternatives like SAP Stock Room Management and SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud, choosing the right one can be challenging.

This blog will explore each of these options and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable solution for your business.

Understanding the Different SAP Warehouse Management Options

1. SAP Stock Room Management

  • What It Is: Stock Room Management is a simplified warehouse management solution introduced within SAP S/4HANA. It offers core warehouse functionalities tailored for businesses with straightforward warehouse needs. SAP created Stock Room Management for customers migrating from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system to S/4HANA, but who do not require the advanced features of SAP EWM.
  • Key Features:
    • Core warehouse processes like stock transfers, goods receipt, and goods issue.
    • Basic inventory management functionalities.
    • Material handling, cycle counting, and internal movements.
    • Simplified user experience with integration into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses that require basic warehouse functionalities without the need for advanced automation or complex workflows. It is ideal for smaller warehouses or companies transitioning from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management system who want to stay within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem without upgrading to full EWM.

2. SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • What It Is: SAP Warehouse Management (WM) on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-based warehouse management functionalities for businesses that want to move their operations to the cloud. It offers core WM features while leveraging the scalability and flexibility of the public cloud infrastructure.
  • Key Features:
    • Cloud-based deployment with access to core warehouse functionalities.
    • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • Real-time inventory visibility and warehouse operations.
    • Regular updates from SAP without the need for heavy customizations.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses looking for a scalable, cloud-based solution to manage basic warehouse operations, especially those that want to reduce infrastructure costs and simplify system management. It is an excellent option for companies transitioning to the cloud or that want a more agile and scalable warehouse management solution.

3. Basic EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Basic EWM is the foundational version of SAP EWM, embedded within SAP S/4HANA. It provides essential warehouse management features with no additional licensing costs.
  • Key Features:
    • Basic warehouse processes such as picking, packing, and goods receipt/goods issue.
    • Inventory management and stock transfers.
    • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless data flow.
  • Best Suited For: Small to medium-sized businesses or warehouses with simple processes that don’t require advanced features like automation or complex workflows.

4. Advanced EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Advanced EWM is the more feature-rich version of SAP EWM, offering a comprehensive set of advanced warehouse management functionalities within SAP S/4HANA.
  • Key Features:
    • Complex warehouse operations, including cross-docking, wave management, slotting, and labor management.
    • Full support for automation systems (AGVs, conveyors).
    • Advanced stock management, including batch management and serialization.
    • Yard management, kitting, and value-added services.
  • Best Suited For: Medium to large businesses with complex warehouse processes that require high levels of automation and optimization.

5. Decentralized EWM

  • What It Is: Decentralized EWM is a stand-alone version of SAP EWM that operates on a separate server from the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, offering greater flexibility and scalability.
  • Key Features:
    • Runs independently from the SAP ERP system for improved flexibility.
    • Comprehensive functionality similar to Advanced EWM.
    • Designed for large warehouses with high transaction volumes and complex operations.
    • Multi-warehouse support and real-time process control.
  • Best Suited For: Large, global enterprises with multiple warehouses, complex operations, and high transaction volumes, where independence from the ERP system is crucial for uninterrupted operations.

Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right SAP Warehouse Management Solution

1. Warehouse Complexity

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are designed for businesses with straightforward warehouse operations. They are ideal if your processes are mostly manual, and you don’t require complex workflows or advanced automation.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer more comprehensive features, such as integration with automation, real-time process monitoring, and optimization. These are best for businesses with intricate warehouse setups, multiple locations, or advanced inventory management needs.
  • SAP WM on S/4HANA Public Cloud is suitable for companies seeking a cloud-first approach, providing core functionalities but with scalability and real-time access to inventory data.

2. Automation Needs

  • If your warehouse relies on automation or is planning to integrate automated systems (e.g., conveyors, AGVs, robots), you will need the capabilities of Advanced EWM or Decentralized EWM. These solutions offer robust support for integrating automation with your warehouse processes.
  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are better suited for manual operations or warehouses with low levels of automation.

3. Cloud Adoption Strategy

  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud is the optimal choice if your business is looking to adopt a cloud-based WMS. It provides flexibility, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure costs, making it ideal for companies focused on digital transformation and scalability.
  • For businesses that prefer an on-premise system or already have SAP S/4HANA in place, Embedded EWM (Basic or Advanced) or Decentralized EWM are viable options.

4. Cost and Licensing

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are included with SAP S/4HANA, making them cost-effective solutions for businesses that don’t need the additional features provided by Advanced or Decentralized EWM.
  • Advanced EWM requires additional licensing, but it offers significantly more functionality for businesses with complex warehouse needs.
  • Decentralized EWM comes with higher costs, as it requires separate infrastructure and licensing, but it is essential for large enterprises that need a fully independent warehouse management system.

5. Future Growth and Scalability

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are suitable for businesses with relatively stable warehouse operations that don’t anticipate significant changes in the near future.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer scalability, making them ideal for growing businesses or enterprises with plans to expand their warehouse operations, implement automation, or add new distribution centers.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-native scalability, making it an excellent choice for companies looking to grow their operations quickly and cost-effectively while leveraging cloud infrastructure.

Conclusion: Which SAP Warehouse Management Solution is Right for You?

Choosing the right SAP warehouse management solution depends on your business’s operational complexity, growth plans, and technological direction. Here's a summary of when each solution may be the best fit:

  • Stock Room Management: Best for businesses with simple warehouse operations that are moving from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system and want to continue with basic functionality within SAP S/4HANA.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud: Ideal for companies focused on adopting cloud solutions with core warehouse functionality and scalability.
  • Basic EWM: Perfect for small to medium-sized businesses that need essential warehouse management features as part of their SAP S/4HANA system without additional costs.
  • Advanced EWM: Suited for medium to large businesses with more complex warehouse operations, requiring advanced automation, inventory management, and process optimization.
  • Decentralized EWM: The best choice for large, multi-warehouse businesses that need a fully independent WMS, capable of handling high volumes and offering real-time, 24/7 operations.

By assessing your current and future warehouse needs, transaction volumes, levels of automation, and growth strategy, you can make an informed decision and choose the right SAP warehouse management solution to drive operational efficiency and support your business objectives.

entry.title: Re: SAP BY Design - UK BACS Domestic Payment Format
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/uk-bacs-domestic-payment-format/qaq-p/13868752

entry.title: Quantity should get calculated automatically in SEN
entry.content:
Hello,

scenario.

PO created with 5 qty with each unit price of Rs.20 so total Rs 100.

Then, we are getting the invoice from the vendor for each unit Rs.25 so now when we are doing the SEN, system should adjust and automatically calculate the QTY.

For example if i enter the gross price in SEN, system should do the math with the 5 Qty.

SAP Business Workflow SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management SAP Supply Chain Management

entry.title: Re: SAP BY Design - UK BACS Domestic Payment Format
entry.content:
Hello,

Looks like you will have to start with the new XSLT transformation. since SAP does not seem to have one.

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/uk-bacs-domestic-payment-format/qaq-p/13868752

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Project Settlement Process
entry.content:
Hi All,

Greetings !

Regarding investment type project settlement , AUC no gets generated upon release of WBS element & defaulted as settlement receiver . Now my doubt is that

1. Is it mandatory to settle  WBS cost frist to AUC or we can directly settle the cost to asset no . 

2. Suppose if we first settlet the cost ( say 1k usd )  to AUC from WBS element & then from AUC to Asset . Will the cost be zero at WBS element & at AUC level after settlement to Asset ?

Thanking you very much .

Best Regards 

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

As a newer SAP MM consultant, I've been employed at SAP MM for the past six months. 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

Because SAP facilitates the process improvement of enterprises, I truly enjoy working with it. It's incredible how much of an impact technology can have. In order to have a fantastic career in this sector, I want to constantly learning and developing.

I'm excited to try out for a free certification because I think it will enable me to study more and advance in my career. I'm aware that technology is a constantly evolving field, so I try to keep up with the most recent developments. I am appreciative of the chance and enthusiastic about the opportunities that lie ahead. I appreciate you taking a look at my request for a free certification try. 

Thanks & Regard

entry.title: Introduction to SAP Customer Onboarding and Embedded Launch Activities for RISE with SAP
entry.content: This session provides an overview of the customer onboarding process and the embedded launch activities designed for RISE with SAP customers. We will discuss key phases and milestones, covering the support structure customers can expect after signing a contract. Attendees will gain insights into the resources, tools, and services available to ensure a seamless transition and successful start with RISE with SAP.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

entry.title: A Beginner's demo for sending data from SOLACE to CPI - 2nd scenario
entry.content:
Please check first part or scenario#1 to send data from CPI to SOLACE using below blog link.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/a-beginner-s-demo-for-pushing-data-from-sap-cpi-to-solace/ba-p/13868012#M169245

Let's see how we can send data from SOLACE to CPI.

Basically, we would need to use Queues from SOLACE to send data to CPI.

SCENARIO 2 – Sending data from SOLACE queue to CPI

As we already are pushing data from Scenario 1 into QUEUE SOurce in SOLACE

We are trying to push same data from QUEUE SOurce to CPI again (CPI iflow)

IFLOW -> HTTPS to END event

Here, we are just testing if we can receive data using the HTTPS endpoint URL.

CPI Setup:

  1. Create a HTTPS sender channel and mention connection data
  2. Provide the HTTPS endpoint to SOLACE to be used for configuration

 

SOLACE setup: to send data from SOLACE queue to CPI HTTPS endpoint mentioned below

https://a8353b8atrial.it-cpitrial05-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/http/RP/SOLACE

OAUTH 2.0 credentials given from BTP instance of Process Integration runtime of type iflow

  1. Navigate to Broker Manager -> Clients -> REST tab

2. Create a RDP – rest Delivery Point for the same

I have created ROHINI as shown above

3. Create Rest Consumer on tab after selecting the newly created RDP

4.  Provide IFLOW end point’s host name here & provide Credentials type & credentials.

     I have provided OAUTH 2.0 details from CPI to connect to the same

Once you Enable TLS & REST delivery point, you can see both consumer & RDP are Up & running

5. Queue binding should be done so that we can push data from this queue to CPI. I am using the same queue used in 1st scenario to reuse the queue for pushing in second flow.

6. Select the queue name & inside that you can see post Request target – HTTP path to send to iflow

TESTING:

  1. Start iflow#1 to push data (CPI to SOLACE IFLOW - to fill the queue) shown in the first blog.
  2. Deploy iflow#2 to start consuming data via iflow#1 from SOLACE

SOLACE -> Queue -> Consumers -> RDP client summary below

Cheers,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: Field Extension Wizard in S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello @Andre_Fischer and @taranam ,

Thanks for the explanation and detailed information.

I followed the blog and created the custom fields via the Eclipse ADT (Developer Extensibility). However, the fields are not visible in the Manage Sales Order V2 App. Could you please let me know how we can enable the fields in the Fiori Elements app?

Kind regards,
Santhosh Rajan

entry.title: Re: Net due date change from bill of lading date
entry.content:
Hi,

As per my knowledge it is not possible to update due date based on BL date, payment terms
facilitate 4 dates as baseline date and in that no option to customized date.

As per your scenario in invoice document maybe you do not print due date, if it the case
then contact to abaper and develop a new aging report. Where you calculate the date
based on BL date with using payment terms days.

Regards,

entry.title: In SAP S/4 HANA International Trade-Intrastat declaration, while defining POI, which plants we enter
entry.content:
In SAP S/4 HANA International Trade-Intrastat declaration, while defining POI, which plants we need to enter under the section titled "Delivering Plants for Intercompany Sales"?

Suitable replies will be amply rewarded with points.

Warm regards,

Deepak

entry.title: Re: Problems with AppState in FIORI-App on Cloud Foundry
entry.content: Could you try to add the app to SAP Build Workzone and try again?

entry.title: CL_GUI_TEXTEDIT - Event on Change Text
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to register an event concering changing the content of a text editor (class CL_GUI_TEXTEDIT)?

Thanks a lot in advance,

Best regards

Reinhold

entry.title: Re: Why Domain Experience is must to be a SAP Functional consultant?
entry.content:
Hello

I'm very excited and glad to read all the suggestions provided in the blog hosted by the Balaji about Why Domain Experience is must for SAP Consultants.

I agree all the terms given in the blog but would like to inform, the basic criteria is must and should which is clearly highlighted for fresh career aspires. But the chances of fulfilling all the criteria to work in SAP projects might be the challenge for Experienced people aswell. bzc those who start SAP career by doing some functional certification after started working in any MNC IT company. They might not had chance before to gain some Domain area Expertise.  Alternatively, they definitely look for Implementation, but every SAP guy must know basic competition to get an SAP Implementation project from any MNC IT company.  So, did Balaji who is the blog host or other who are part of blog readers can comment on this area of Expertise How to gain; mostly in SAP skills like SD, MM, FICO, SCM, CRM etc.  Also, people like me seeking for opportunities to work with SAP Implementations at branded countries like EU region or US or any other similar country.  How to compete in this global market to gain those requisite High-Cost billing Implementation Knowledge, that will put a Resume into work with Senior leaders in the company management.  

 

Thanks,

Arava Santosh

entry.title: Re: Types of customer projects?
entry.content:
Hi @DP_SAP ,

Can you please refer to the below SAP Help Documentation for the same
Customer and Internal Project Management
Scope item for customer Project Management - J11
Scope item for internal Project Management -  1A8

Regards,

Monisha.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I'm Thang Bui (Ethan) from Vietnam. Currently, I'm working as a SAP Technical Consultant. I'm excited about the digital skills initiative and I am very excited about the prospect of earning an SAP certification. I have been following the latest developments in SAP technology and am impressed by its potential to revolutionize business processes. I am committed to investing the time and effort required to successfully complete the certification program.

Thank you for considering my application for the free certification attempt.

entry.title: Re: i need to update the tax code for all or multiple lin...
entry.content: Search Tax Code Determination and In Purchase field , Select the tax code as per your needs.

entry.title: Re: Controlling Double Click event in PAI
entry.content:
Thank u Carlos. I was becoming crazy...

Gracias Carlos, llevaba horas tirandome de los pelos ;).

entry.title: Excel Multi-Chapter Content Refresh
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
Where we are sourcing all the reports and schedules from an Excel file – is there a way to refresh all the Excel data cache chapters at once? One at a time is very inconvenient and time consuming.
I tried running content refresh from the chapter tab and I get a message that “Missing data source in Administration > Content Refresh > Datasources.

I see the server-side content refresh and see the administrator settings but thought this is no longer supported.
We are using Stack 21.

The note 2935984 - DM - Content refresh fails due to Excel data-source says you can only refresh on the client – does that mean the user has to go into each DC and refresh?
If so, possible option is not import most of the data to one DC chapter and then refresh each of the Chapters from there?
There has to be a better way? Otherwise - … More links… and more refreshes…client will be very unhappy.

Thanks
Eyal

entry.title: Which device type to use to print Adobe Forms and ZPL output in a Zebra printer
entry.content:
Which device type to use to print Adobe Forms and ZPL output in a Zebra printer?

Currently we are interchanging device types from AZPL203 to print PDF output labels from SAP adobe forms to ZLB_ZEB to print native ZPL output.

Is there a single device type we can use to print PDF and ZPL data types? Is there any workaround on this issue aside from changing device type?

Hi @DP_SAP ,

Can you please refer to the below SAP Help Documentation for the same

Scope item for internal Project Management -  1A8

Regards,

Monisha.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
• SAP Managed Tags: SAP Universal ID, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance , FIN Controlling  , SAP Profitability and Performance Management 

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Eman Kassem  and I am truly excited about the opportunity to join the Digital Skills Initiative. i have been working in linux-plus in Egypt as sap FICO consultant for the past year. Throughout my career, I've recognized the immense value of staying up-to-date with digital advancements and expanding my skill set in this fast-paced world. sharpening my technical expertise and staying competitive in the evolving digital landscape. I am confident that this initiative will not only enhance my current capabilities but also empower me to contribute more effectively to the digital transformation efforts within my organization and beyond.

Thank you for considering my application, and I look forward to the possibility of joining this incredible learning journey. Please let me know how I can proceed with the registration process.
I really need this certificate and i can't afford it so it would be appreciated if you support.

Best regards,

@David_Chaviano 

Labels: Certification  SAP Profitability and Performance Management 


 Tags:  About David_Chaviano - SAP Community

entry.title: New Feature Release SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the new feature release 2407 of SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition (Build Version 1.21.1).

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition is the most comprehensive real-time security event management and monitoring solution in the cloud, tailored for SAP S/4HANA applications.

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection helps safeguard enterprises' most valuable assets to prevent financial, legal, and reputational damage, ensuring business continuity by protecting SAP applications. It also streamlines audits and manages regulatory compliance efficiently, such as GDPR Article 30, while simplifying suspicious user activity analysis to create transparency and providing the speed needed to analyze vast amounts of data for timely, informed decision-making.

Closing the security talent gap, the solution is provided together with a managed security service by SAP or by trusted partners. Hence, customers do not need larger investments in dedicated resources or in building up teams having deep security knowledge.

Key Features and Business Values release 2407

Let’s dive into the key features and the benefits for customers and partners:

Create custom tailored patterns:
By enabling the creation of custom patterns to trigger alerts for suspicious customer-specific activities, businesses can individually enhance their security posture, tailor threat detection to their unique operational environment, and reduce the risk of undetected breaches. This leads to increased protection of valuable assets and ensures greater business continuity.

New forensic lab:
Having the ability to perform additional deep dive security analytics, such as Threat Hunting and Forensic User- and Environment Behavioral Analysis, SAP Enterprise Threat Detection empowers businesses to proactively identify and mitigate potential threats before they escalate. This capability not only strengthens the overall security framework but also improves incident response times, enhances operational efficiency, and provides a deeper understanding of user and environment behaviors, thereby ensuring sustained business continuity and resilience. Additionally, it supports regulatory requirements such as GDPR Article 30, further protecting the business from legal and financial consequences.

New S/4HANA extractor framework:
Enabling the use of the new Extraction Framework in S/4HANA systems provides businesses with more detailed log data extraction configurations and higher performance. This enhancement allows for more precise data monitoring and faster analysis, leading to improved security incident detection and response times. As a result, businesses benefit from increased operational efficiency, better protection of their critical assets, and a stronger security posture, ultimately supporting business continuity and compliance.

Central monitoring app for partner:
The ability for partners to use a central Partner App for monitoring multiple customers within a single UI delivers significant business value for both customers and service providers. For customers, this means enhanced security oversight, as their environments can be monitored more efficiently and comprehensively by trusted experts. For service providers, it streamlines the monitoring process, reduces operational overhead, and allows for scalable service delivery. Additionally, similar attacks in different customer environments can be recognized more easily, facilitating quicker threat detection and response. This centralized approach ultimately leads to improved customer satisfaction, stronger security postures, and a more resilient business ecosystem.

Continuous delivery of content:
Continuous delivery of content, such as patterns raising alerts, into the product provides substantial business value for both customers and service providers. Customers benefit from always having the latest, most effective security measures at their disposal without the need for manual updates, ensuring their systems are protected against emerging threats. Service providers can leverage these continuously updated patterns to deliver more comprehensive and proactive security services. This ongoing content delivery enhances the overall security posture, reduces the risk of breaches, and ensures compliance with evolving regulatory standards. Ultimately, this leads to increased trust and satisfaction among customers, and a more dynamic and responsive service capability for providers.

Call to action:

Contact our team to learn how you can benefit from this efficient solution!

Useful links:

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection | SIEM and Cybersecurity

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection | SAP Community

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Edition | SAP Help Portal

Kind regards,

Arndt Lingscheid

Global Solution Owner Cyber Security and Data Protection at SAP

entry.title: GST Planned Delivery Cost
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We are using Non deductible taxes in Purchase order with planned delivery cost.

We are using subtotal 4 and routine 362 

In this case when we post the Goods receipt below is the accounting entries are post.

730609000    KBS        Line Access. - Cons     2,596.00

420300000    WRX        GR/IR    2,396.00-
420300000     FR1        GR/IR    200.00-

in this case PO Qty is 20 and net price is 100

planned delivery cost is 10 INR per Quantity  total 200

Taxes 18% GST

my Client requirement is GST on planned delivery cost should not be added in WRX at the time of GRN, it should be add in FRI

730609000    KBS        Line Access. - Cons     2,596.00

420300000    WRX        GR/IR    2,360.00-
420300000     FR1        GR/IR    236.00-

requesting you to help how can we post above entry

entry.title: Re: PoC with SAP Business Network Purchase Order PDF with Document Information Extraction (AI Servic
entry.content: Best Wishes 👍

entry.title: oModel.create is not a function
entry.content:
This is my controller for adding data in backend through UI , but i am getting error oModel.create is not a function 
onOpenDialog: function () {
            if (!this._oDialog) {
                this._oDialog = new Dialog({
                    title: "Add New Product",
                    content: [
                        new VBox({
                            items: [
                                new Input("dialogInputID", {
                                    placeholder: "Enter Product ID"
                                }),
                                new Input("dialogInputName", {
                                    placeholder: "Enter Product Name"
                                }),
                                new Input("dialogInputDescription", {
                                    placeholder: "Enter Description"
                                }),
                                new Input("dialogInputReleaseDate", {
                                    placeholder: "Enter Release Date"
                                })
                            ]
                        })
                    ],
                    beginButton: new Button({
                        text: "Submit",
                        press: this.onAddProduct.bind(this)
                    }),
                    endButton: new Button({
                        text: "Cancel",
                        press: function () {
                            this._oDialog.close();
                        }.bind(this)
                    })
                });

                this.getView().addDependent(this._oDialog);
            }

            this._oDialog.open();
        },

        onAddProduct: function () {
            // Retrieve data from dialog inputs
            var oID = sap.ui.getCore().byId("dialogInputID").getValue();
            var oName = sap.ui.getCore().byId("dialogInputName").getValue();
            var oDescription = sap.ui.getCore().byId("dialogInputDescription").getValue();
            var oReleaseDate = sap.ui.getCore().byId("dialogInputReleaseDate").getValue();
       
            // Validate inputs
            if (!oID || !oName || !oDescription || !oReleaseDate) {
                sap.m.MessageToast.show("Please fill in all fields.");
                return;
            }
       
            // Create new product object
            var oNewProduct = {
                ID: parseInt(oID), // Ensure ID is an integer
                Name: oName,
                Description: oDescription,
                ReleaseDate: oReleaseDate // Format date as required
            };
       
            // Get the OData model
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel("ODataM1");
       
            if (oModel) {
                // Create a new entry in the backend
                oModel.create("/Products", oNewProduct, {
                    success: function () {
                        console.log("Product added successfully!"); // Debugging statement
                        sap.m.MessageToast.show("Product added successfully!");
                       
                        // Close the dialog
                        if (this._oDialog) {
                            this._oDialog.close(); // Close the dialog
                        } else {
                            console.error("Dialog instance is not available.");
                        }
       
                        // Refresh the table to display new data
                        var oTable = this.getView().byId("productTable");
                        if (oTable) {
                            oTable.getBinding("items").refresh(); // Refresh the table binding
                        }
                    }.bind(this),
                    error: function (oError) {
                        console.error("Error adding product:", oError);
                        sap.m.MessageToast.show("Error adding product.");
                    }
                });
            } else {
                console.error("The OData model is not instantiated or is not available.");
            }
        },

entry.title: Re: HOW to securing odata services or api in sap btp CAPM...
entry.content: I think https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/security/overview should be a good starting point.

entry.title: Key Learnings Opportunities in the SAP Ariba Roadmap
entry.content:
Implementing a product is always a challenge. It requires building a project team, planning, configuring, customizing, integrating, testing, realigning, and meeting business requirements – all while adhering to deadlines – until you finally deploy your new application. An often overlooked aspect of implementation is continuous learning. Either self-enablement, instructor-led, or learning workshops should take place during the implementation weeks, so that you have everyone in the team ready to take on their responsibilities when the product goes live.

In this blog, will guide you through the key learning opportunities in the SAP Ariba Roadmap, from the Discover phase to the Run phase, and explain what you can expect from each.

Identify and prepare learning opportunities for the Project Team

Before diving into trainings, it's crucial to develop a comprehensive plan enlightening what each stakeholder needs to learn, the methods for delivering these trainings and how to track progress. The main tasks are:

  • Identify Learning Needs for Project Team
  • Identify Learning Needs Identification Process
  • Create Learning Curriculum based on SAP Standard Learning
  • Create Learning Curriculum.

 Once you have this plan in place, you can implement it by utilizing several different resources. You can use the SAP Activate accelerators provided in the Roadmap, propose relevant courses available on Learning Hub (Ensure Project Team Members Access to the SAP Learning Hub), offer workshops, or create your own way to share content through documents, guidelines, and others.

Ensure that your training offers are well-organized and effective by confirming that the stakeholders have the tools they will need to proceed. If you are offering an on-site workshop remember to Plan Learning Logistics, i.e. making sure the space designated is comfortable and favorable to learning. On the other hand, if you are offering online courses, verify if a suitable technological tool (i.e. computer or cellphone), and a good internet connection are available, as well as access to the needed platform. Besides that, make sure you Deliver Enablement to the trainers and that the content is up-to-date.

 Organizational Change Management

The first set of training you will encounter is related to Organizational Change Management (OCM). Their intent is to help you Comprehend the Approach to Organizational Change Management and Understand the Fundamentals of Organizational Change Management.

 Fit-to-standard

During the Prepare phase you will be participating in Fit-to-standard workshops, which intend to present the standard SAP Best Practices and identify changes in their content. To prepare, review the documents listed in the Self-Enable on the Fit-to-Standard Process task, which will provide you with an overview of the process, how it develops during each phase,  how it will support your project, and how the workshop will be conducted.

Supplier and buyer enablement

The supplier enablement activities begin in the Explore phase, where you will be instructed on onboarding, empowerment, communication, strategy planning, integration, and other related topics. The key tasks are:

  • Conduct Supplier Onboarding and Enablement Overview
  • Conduct Supplier Integration Overview
  • Conduct Supplier Communication Workshop
  • Conduct Supplier Data Workshop.

 In the Realize phase you will focus on the  Supplier Enablement deliverable. It will help you Define Supplier Training Strategy and Plan and plan subsequent actions in this or in other phases, such as Supplier Learning (Deploy phase) and Conduct Supplier Enablement Activities (Run phase).

The buyer enablement follows the same approach and is closely related to the content you will create, instruct, or deliver to the customer and the end-user. The main information can be found in the task Conduct Standard Account Buyer Best Practices Workshop and the deliverable Buyer Enablement.

 As you complete your SAP implementation, remember that success depends on ongoing training. It can ensure a smooth transition, allows you to keep on top of your product even after the implementation process ends, and enables you to stay adaptable and achieve continued success.

entry.title: Quantity is not updating in Contract/outline agreement when we create SO with reference to Billing.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Due to quantity descripency we need to create a sales order with reference to Billing where qty should be update in contract as well.

Process flow:

Contract/outline Agreement: 1000 qty

Sales Order: 100 qty

Delivery: 100 qty

Billing 100 qty

Order with reference to above billing: 20 qty

The actual qty was delivered to customer less than sales order qty. 

Actual Qty Delivered: 80 qty

Sales Order created with: 100 qty,      Delivery : 100 qty,      Billing: 100 qty 

Second order created with reference to Billing for remaining 20 qty.

Once customer received the the qty and invoice he got only 80 qty instead of 100 qty in Billing. now we need to deliver 20 more qty to customer. for the tracking purpose we are creating our sales order with reference to billing/invoice which was sent to customer, we are changing qty in order with remaining 20 qty and processing further .. now problem is that 20 qty are not reflecting in our contract/outline agreement.

After creating first SO with 100 qty  remaining quantity in contract/agreement  was 900, and after creating second order with 20 qty  (created with reference to Billing) I am expecting 20 qty will also reduce and remaining qty 880 will be in contract/agreemet BUT contract/agreemet is still with 900 qty and there is no changes in qty in contract/agreement after creating sales order with Billing.

I want to achieve it when sales order is created with reference to Billing, 20 qty should update contract/Agreement.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Cant Delete Member ID from Account Dimension in SAP A...
entry.content: It says

entry.title: Re: Errors with Business Objects 4.3 Sp3 Patch 12
entry.content: Hi James,

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM – Master data objects
entry.content:
In my last blog post on Key User Extensibility in BRIM (SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management), the focus was on transactional data.

Now, I want to shift the focus to the extension of BRIM master data objects. Specially, we will take a closer look at the Contract Account and the Provider Contract Item.

Content:

  • Business Context Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship
  • Business Context Provider Contract Item
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

Business Context “Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship”

This business context enables the creation of a custom field for the Contract Account Partner Relationship. Our example field MTCaExt has the type Text (10), and we have made it available in the user interfaces and SOAP APIs.

In addition, we activated the Business Scenario Contract Account Partner Relationship to Contract Accounting Business Partner Item.

The sample custom field

Since we enabled the User Interfaces, our sample field is now included in the relevant apps. It appears in the Customer Fields section on the General Data tab. You can maintain this field using both the Creation and the Change apps for the Contract Account object.

The sample field on the Contract Account object

As you already know from the previous blog, a Business Scenario enables the automatic transfer of data between objects. The diagram below illustrates the available Business Scenarios for Business Context Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship.

Overview available Business Scenarios

When a FI-CA document is posted, the value from the Contract Account master data object is transferred to the FI-CA document and displayed in the user interface as we have activated the scenario for Business Partner Items. The business partner item view now has its own tab for Customer Data, where the field is shown.

Custom field transferred from Contract Account to BP item

The field is also available in the data structures of the Contract Account object used in the relevant Cloud BAdIs or function modules in on-premise systems. For example, consider FKK_EVENT_8135: The structure CAPARTNER – Contract Account Partner-Specific is now extended with our custom field and you can use the information in your own implementation of the BAdI.

The sample field in the import parameter of BAdI FKK_EVENT_8135

The field is now part of the service’s WSDL, as we have enabled the field for the SOAP APIs. In this example, we extended the FKCMANAGECONTRACTACCOUNT service. The response of the ContractAccountRetrieve operation now includes our custom field, YY1_MTCaExt. The create and update operations of this service have also been extended.

The sample field in the response of FKCMANAGECONTRACTACCOUNT

There are many features more to explore. Why don’t you try them out? 😊

Business Context “Provider Contract Item”

The Provider Contract Item object can be extended with custom fields by using the corresponding business context. The example field MTPcExt is of type Text(10) and enabled for SOAP API usage, in the user interfaces and APIs. As an example for automatic data transfer between objects, we activate the Provider Contract Item to Contract Accounting Business Partner Item scenario.

Enablement of SOAP APIs

All the business scenarios available for data transfer are illustrated in the graphic below. Of particular interest is the scenario Provider Contract Item to FI-CA / RAR Transfer Record, which can be highly useful in BRIM to RAR integrations.

Overview available Business Scenarios

In scenarios involving external management of the provider contract object, you may use SOAP API FKCMANAGEPROVIDERCONTRACT. This API supports the use of fields created via Custom Fields app. The screenshot below provides a schematic visualization of the ProviderContractCreate-Request.

Schematic provider contract create request with the custom field

You can now display the information in the provider contract display app by adding it to the layout of the provider contract item:

Display provider contract item with the custom field

During the posting process of a FI-CA document that references this provider contract item, the value of your custom field is transferred to the business partner item.

Custom field transferred from Provider Contract Item to BP item

You may recall that we have also activated the APIs for this custom field. We will now validate its effect on the CDS view I_ProviderContractItem using our View Browser app. The definition of this CDS view includes our example field, which you can use when consuming the view.

Definition of view I_ProviderContractItem

Conclusion

With this article and the previous one about transactional data, you should now have a solid foundation for your first steps in the area of Key User Extensibility in BRIM. We encourage you to explore the features and learn how to utilize them; you will quickly see their benefits.

Support our common mission to keeping the S/4HANA core clean! 😊

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields
  • F2170 – View Browser

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

entry.title: From CAD to Manufacturing as-a-Service. New Idea-to-Market Public Cloud Scenario available
entry.content:
Overview

The journey started in the mid-1990s with SAP R/3 and the CPI-C based CAD Interfaces, then over to iPPE, CAD Desktop, SAP PLM 7, SAP ECTR and S/4HANA and now to the new SAP PLM – SAP has constantly been supporting customers in their product development and market introduction efforts. Since the first releases we not only focused on typical PLM topics like CAD data management but also always provided an integrated path into the rest of the enterprise like purchasing or manufacturing.

Software for the Public Cloud now allows additional customer groups like growing enterprises, subsidiaries, country organizations or individual departments to benefit, … especially since we recently introduced a missing key capability in the public cloud-based SAP PLM - the new ‘Product Data Integration’ Software-as-a-Service application for public cloud-based CAD Integration. Together with this CAD Integration, we also provided all necessary enhancements to allow a smooth handover of product structures, routings, and products to SAP S/4HANA Cloud supporting downstream processes like purchasing and manufacturing.

What’s the benefit?

For the first time SAP is now offering a completely public cloud-based suite to support all processes relevant from early product definition and development through to manufacturing. As with all public cloud services, the effort that you need for implementing the software and keeping it up to date is reduced dramatically. In addition, you directly receive all the new features and developments – and not just years later. Several extensibility tools continue to allow you to enhance the software, always following the clean core guidance. Altogether, this makes it easier, especially for smaller units, to get started with software support for your product development processes.

Idea-to-Market

To lower the entry barrier for the implementation of SAP PLM, this all should be as simple as possible – especially since this additional deployment option mainly targets smaller units and smaller implementation partners. To provide additional guidance for the implementation, we now deliver process blueprints on SAP’s Business Accelerator Hub. On the Business Accelerator Hub, you can find reference content for all areas in an enterprise: https://api.sap.com/allprocess , documented by us following the same methodology. You’ll find all information for product development under ‘Idea to Market’. The new solution variant ‘Standard Engineering (Discreet Industries) for Cloud Deployment’ is now available here: https://api.sap.com/package/StandardEngineeringDiscreteIndustriesforPublicCloudDeployment/businessprocess. The four categories of diagrams we provide help you to understand with typical enterprise architecture views which business process scope is covered, which components are involved, how is the data flowing through the integrated system landscape, and more:

  • The value flow diagram depicts all business activities supported by the variant
  • The component diagram depicts all solution components needed to implement the business process
  • The data flow diagram depicts key flows of master and transactional data of the variant
  • The process flow diagram, based on the BPMN standard, depicts the process flow and used to specify the collaboration of the different solution components in more detail

From the big picture down to the more detailed process flow diagrams, you as our partners and customers can use this as a template for your implementations.

Several aspects like project management, prototyping, time confirmation will be picked up in future releases. Also engineer-to-order or configure-to-order will be covered in detail later.

What is covered by Idea-to-Market?

The described process starts with an idea for a new product or an improvement resulting from feedback or an ideation campaign. Sometimes you get several similar ideas. Therefore, several ideas can be aggregated into single needs. The ideas and needs are then linked to requirements. With the requirements structuring, a product manager has a clear overview as a basis for a transparent product planning process. From there (mostly for more complex products) either a systems model or a high-level product structure can be directly created. Additionally, CAD engineers can start working on the detailed design and with this also defining the bill-of-material.

Of course, there can be many iterations until the best design is available – these iterations can be managed with SAP PLM’s versioning concept of products and product structures. The built-in visualization service, configuration management, and collaboration features additionally support the development process. Once a certain maturity level has been reached the handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can be started. Only then the missing material masters, routings and bills-of-materials are created in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system for further processing.

In the next weeks we will also roll out this reference flow to SAP Cloud ALM and the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Summary

SAP offers simplified access to idea-to-market support in the public cloud, especially for smaller or growing companies or units. Availability of detailed process templates helps our customers and partners reduce project risk and accelerate the project implementation. With the new ‘Idea-to-Market: Standard Engineering (Discrete Industries) for Public Cloud Deployment’, you now have a template process description available. Over time, additional content and variants (ETO, CTO or process industries) will be added as well.

Links:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub: Business Processes: https://api.sap.com/allprocess
  • Idea-to-Market: https://api.sap.com/process/ideaToMarket
  • Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/I2M?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Errors with Business Objects 4.3 Sp3 Patch 12
entry.content: Hi James, Tried the KBA. However, issue still exists.

entry.title: Extensibility of Thailand Withholding Tax Report - 50BIS
entry.content:
Scenario

The requirement is to display type of service for each type of tax displayed at point 5 in Thailand Withholding tax report 50BIS.

As per standard layout provided by government (50BIS_Thai (rd.go.th)), input to display such information is not present, hence report generated from DRC's standard report will also not have such details. 

However, required details (which is coming from tax code description) are exposed in service - FDP_FIWTTH_50BIS_SRV with variables named withholdingtaxcodename51, withholdingtaxcodename52 and withholdingtaxcodename53.

We can use these fields directly in mapping of adobe forms as per their requirements.

Steps

Download existing layout and change it as per requirement.

  • Open App 'Maintain Form Templates'.

Figure 1: Maintain Form Template

  • Search With 'FIWTTH_50BIS' under pre-delivered templates.

Figure 2: Search Standard Form

  • Select form and download it.

Figure 3: Download Standard Form

  • Modify form as per custom requirement (using Adobe LiveCycle Designer) to display withholding tax code descriptions for tax lines displayed at point 5.
  • Variables withholdingtaxcodename51, withholdingtaxcodename52 and withholdingtaxcodename53 are exposed which stores withholding tax code descriptions for tax items displayed in same sequence.
  • As part of this demo, we will do concatenation of all 3 values and showing it along with point5's existing description.

Upload customized Form Template

  • Open same app - 'Maintain Form Templates' and click new under Custom Templates.

Figure 4: Upload Custom Form Template

  • Provide custom form name and description. Select Data source as 'FDP_FIWTTH_50BIS_SRV', Form type as 'Content' and Available countries/regions as 'Thailand' and Upload modified form.

Figure 5: Custom Form details

Create/Extend Report Definition

  • First, we will create copy of existing standard report definition 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1' in custom object and will make required modifications in it. You can extend existing report rather than copying and modifying.
  • Open App - 'Define Statutory Reports'.

Figure 6: Define Statutory Reports

  • Search report 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1' and click copy, provide custom report definition name to and save.

Figure 7: Copy Existing Report

  • Click on custom report definition name to navigate inside.

Figure 8: Navigate to Custom Report Definition

  • Scroll down to Section 'Correspondence Definition'. We can see standard Correspondence with Form Template 'FIWTTH_50BIS'. We have to replace this with custom form which we created earlier. We cannot directly edit standard form details, so delete existing one and create new custom correspondence (Before deleting, save other existing details like query id, output type and parameters so that in while creating custom correspondence we can input those fields as it is).
  • Select existing correspondence and delete using button given on toolbar. 

Figure 9: Delete Existing Correspondence Definition

  • Add new custom correspondence. Provide Correspondence ID, Form Template name, which was created earlier, Query ID as 'TAX_ITEM', Correspondence Name and Output Type as 'ACCT_WHT' and click OK.

Figure 10: Add new Correspondence Definition

  • Once created, click on it to navigate inside where we need to map parameters. Map it as shown in below picture.

Figure 11: Correspondence ID Parameters

  • Save it, go back and active Report definition.
  • You can find more details about report definition of SAP Help portal - Creating a Report Definition | SAP Help Portal

Create/Extend Report Category

  • Create/Extend Reporting category to include newly created Report definition under Reporting activity. Use App 'Define Statutory Reports' for the same.
  • While creating a new report category, refer to the existing standard report category 'TH_WHT_50BIS' and activity ID 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1'. Keep all parameter values, configurations, and settings identical to those in the standard report definition under the reporting activity, with the only change being the new report definition name for the Activity ID.
  • To add new activity, Make use of 'Assign' button.
  • For this demo, new report category is created and added newly created report definition as reporting activity, it will look like below.

Figure 12: Create Report Category

  • You can find more details about Report category of SAP Help portal - Creating a Report Category | SAP Help Portal

Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting

  • After successfully creating report category, need to configure it in reporting entity. You can create new entity or can use existing standard reporting entity used for Thailand Withholding tax reports - TH_WHT_ENT.
  • Add the newly created report category to the reporting entity and configure all relevant settings such as Periodicity, Properties, Parameters, and Organization units, as applicable. Use the standard configurations of the reporting entity 'TH_WHT_ENT' and report category ID 'TH_WHT_50BIS' as a reference. The only change will be replacing the report category name with the custom name. 
    • Public Cloud Customers can access this configuration using SSCUI - 101071.
    • Private Cloud Customers can access this configuration using transaction code SM34 => select View Cluster SRFVC_RPG_ENT.
  • For this demo, new report category is added in standard reporting entity and did all related configs, it will look like below.

Figure 13: Setting Up Statutory Reporting

  • You can find more details about Report category of SAP Help portal - Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting | SAP Help Portal

Configuration of Form Template ID

  • To Configure the Form Template ID, proceed as follows:
    • Public Cloud Customers - Open SSCUI - 102313.
    • Private Cloud Customers - In Customizing for Cross-Application Components, go to Output Control > Assign Form Template.
  • In the Assign Form Template activity, choose New Entries.
  • In the New Entries: Overview of Added Entries page, enter the field values as mentioned below.
    1. In Application Object Type, enter SRF_REPRUN.
    2. In Output Type, enter ACCT_WHT.
    3. In Form Type, choose Output Forms (Gateway interface). (If it's present).
    4. In Form Template ID, enter custom form name. 

Figure 14: Assign Form Template

Generating the Withholding Tax - 50BIS Report using Run Statutory Reports

  • Before running the reports, make sure your user has all authorization to access required business objects like company code, vendors / customers, business place etc. and to execute newly created reports.
  • Run newly generate report and verify generated output.
  • Below is output generated using standard report where tax code description is not present.

Figure 15: Output Generated with Standard Report

  • Below is output generated using custom report, as we can see tax code description (service type) is printed as per custom form layout.

Figure 16: Output Generated with Custom Report

Summary

Figure 17: Summary

entry.title: Upgrade Your Process Mining Skills with the SIGNAL Cookbook
entry.content:
If you've already heard of SIGNAL, the SAP Signavio Analytics Language, you probably know that it's a specialized query language optimized for performing process analysis on large volumes of business process data.

Maybe you've already seen examples of SIGNAL's SQL-like syntax. Perhaps you've already written some simple queries and now want to explore deeper, learning how to write more complex code that unlocks the language's full potential. 

If so, then the SIGNAL Cookbook is for you.

The SIGNAL Cookbook is a learning resource for users who already have some basic familiarity with SIGNAL and want to further develop their abilities with the language. It contains a series of examples, each of which you can consider as a recipe showing how SIGNAL can be used to achieve a specific analytical goal. Each recipe builds up a query step-by-step, guiding you carefully through the process of crafting the code and ensuring you understand the result. 

These recipes are organized into categories corresponding to typical process analysis activities, such as determining cycle times, process conformance, process variants, or rework. This way, you can use the cookbook not only as a learning resource, but also as a browsable reference guide that contains a rich collection of examples adaptable for your own purposes. 

You can find the SIGNAL Cookbook on the SAP Help Portal. And remember: we'll continue to update the cookbook with additional recipes in the future, so check back regularly. If you think there is a must-know recipe not yet described, leave a comment and you might find it in a future update. 

Screenshot of a typical recipe

entry.title: Re: How can I create smart forms/ Labels in S4HANA Public...
entry.content:
Hello @gowthamigunji2000 ,

Apart from what @luizapereira described about custom field.

For logos/text use app 'Manage Logos' and 'Manage Texts'. Refer to the set-up instructions of scope 1LQ.

And, for barcode or QR codes it will be a custom field (text) which is when inserted should be bound to a data field just like any other custom field. Refer to below blog and note as well.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2722969/E 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-output-management-barcodes-and-qr-codes-on-forms/ba-p/12843878

Thanks and Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Use of EDOC transactions to manually import supplier invoice?
entry.content:
Hello eDoc experts,

I am looking for a pre-existing solution that would allow us to manually load a supplier invoice which is in XML format and coded according to the en16931 standard into our SAP ERP EHP8 in order to create the logistics invoice verifiction document. At the moment the users do manual MIRO transation from printed PDF which is obviously tedious; my hope is that we could semi-automatically process the XML files and at least pre-populate the invoice verification data.

If I understand the documentation correctly, the use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance can indeed be used for that (plus a lot more than I am looking for).

Before I go down the path of implementing all the notes needed for the electronic document processing framework (e.g. 3403461 and all that follows), I wanted to find out whether this would indeed give me what I am looking for meaning: use the various EDOC transaction like  EDOC_INBOUND_UPLOAD,  EDOC_INBOUND_MSG or EDOC_COCKPIT to achieve the above stated aim?

I'm not looking for automatic import of invoices from or any of the other fancy stuff. Just a way to process in the XML file and without having to write our own upload program to achieve it?

Also, is there additional licencing associated with using this?

Much appreciated.

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Create task in My Timesheet for EPPM Projects for uns...
entry.content:
Hi @GBo1 ,

To create a task For eppm project in manage my timesheet app, a resource demand should be created.

You can create Resource Demand using Manage Project Demand App.
If employees are assigned to a demand are automatically provided with a corresponding task in the Manage My Timesheet app. If the WBS element has been released and the demand has the status Requested, the task is available in the task list of these employees during the period defined by the start and end dates of the demand. It can be used to post times against the WBS element, cost center, and activity type of the demand. Resources that are not assigned to the demand need to manually create a corresponding task to record times that are related to the demand. 

Regards,
Monisha.

entry.title: Inquiry for Workflow APIs
entry.content:
Hi Team

 

Is there any APIs are available to connect SMARTDOCS workflow which embedded in SAP.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: crystal report viewer not showing images
entry.content: Sort of, open CR 2020 Designer and that report, then set location to your XML file and refresh the report. Do you see the images? If you don't then it's a XML file issue. If you do see the images then save that report with data and then open it in your app and preview without refreshing. Do you see the images?

entry.content: The following appears when I click on the link. No data Unable to fetch data at the moment.

entry.title: Re: Difference between Accounting and Tax Register
entry.content: Thank you Sanil, agreed that this is a custom report and we resolved it through debugging

entry.title: DMEE XML file missing nodes
entry.content:
I maintain a copy of standard CGI_XML_CT tree, where I customize some nodes to comply with local bank requirements.
Some nodes do not appear in output xml file at all.
Example: CdtrAgt->FinInstnId->ClrSysMmbId->MmbId. The node is defined as reference to structure field FPAYH-ZBNKL with status 1-Mandatory.
The tree is activated without errors, in F110 no errors appear during payment medium creation. In table REGUH I can see that field ZBNKL is correctly populated. Despite of this, the node is not contained in the file. Same result if I change the status to Standard.
There is no exit function for this node, no conditions.
What else controlls, if a node is tranferred (even blank - without value) to the payment medium?
Within CdtrAgt->FinInstnId there is also the node BIC, mapped to FPAYH-ZSWIF. This node is transferred to an output file without problems.

thanks for any hint

entry.title: Re: Creation of custom field in master data using Custom Field Fiori application in S/4 HANA UPA 202
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing.

Nice blog.

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content: No, CR for VS will install in VS 2010-> 2022. Note, don't install the MSI packages separately, the installer will do this for you and it confuses VS if both are installed.

entry.title: Re: Why I can not use date/time dimension as linked dimension in optimized mode?
entry.content: Hello, SAP-help confirms this limit (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/7b9defa34dc34c0881dee863e6b4e5c5.html#blending-support-for-sap-bw-live-data-connections-in-optimized-story). Unfortunately there isn't mentioned a reason for it. I hope linking on time dimensions will be possible in the future. Currently there is no such point on the roadmap (https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&PRODUCT=67838200100800006884#Q3%202024).

entry.title: Re: Hide the "post on account " tab in post outgoing payments .
entry.content: Hiding tab is not working in post outgoing payments . For another screens like migo, flavor manager have scripts option. But post outgoing payments tile ,can't have that. And i can't even edit the screen itself. We are searching for another possible ways to solve this. Pls guide us.

entry.title: Business Process Model Connector (BPMC) Sync Solman 7.2 >>Signavio
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to synchronize SAP Solution Manager 7.2 with SAP Signavio. As a baseline, I wanted to import all process steps and associated objects, such as T-codes and Fiori applications from Solution Documentation into Signavio to streamline the modeling process. The process steps and documentation are synchronized, but the objects attached to the process steps do not seem to be copied.

Is it true that the objects do not get copied during the synchronization process? Could someone share their experience or provide insights on this?

Thank you.
